programme.title: The National
programme.desc: CBC's flagship nightly news program, featuring the day's top stories with in-depth and original journalism; Adrienne Arsenault and Ian Hanomansing anchor.

programme.title: CBC News Special: Carney's New Cabinet
programme.desc: Coverage of the swearing-in of Mark Carney's new government at a time of economic uncertainty.

programme.desc: What's happening across Canada and around the world.

programme.desc: Andrew Chang expands the understanding of the stories everybody's talking about.

programme.desc: W5 investigates the identity of a would-be bank robber who exploded in a dramatic heist.

programme.desc: A special hour looking at Charles III's personal relationships – from his mother, Queen Elizabeth II, the late Princess Diana and his sons.

programme.title: W5: Avery Haines Investigates
programme.desc: How far would you go to save your son? Avery Haines investigates why counter terrorism experts are warning of a ticking time bomb.

programme.title: W5
programme.desc: W5 investigates the identity of a would-be bank robber who exploded in a dramatic heist.

programme.title: CTV National News
programme.desc: CTV National News

programme.title: CTV News: Your Canada
programme.desc: Stories that showcase what makes Canada unique.

programme.title: CTV News Special: Rick Hansen: Unbreakable 50 Years Later
programme.desc: Rick Hansen returns to the scene of the accident that changed his life forever. CTV’s Sandie Rinaldo talks to the Canadian icon.

programme.title: CTV National News
programme.desc: CTV National News

programme.title: CTV News: Your Canada
programme.desc: Stories that showcase what makes Canada unique.

programme.title: W5
programme.desc: A boy's disappearance leads to a community turning on his parents.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.sub-title: Japanese Food Inspired by Other Cultures
programme.desc: Chef Hana makes chicken wings, Japanese curry and rice and chawanmushi - a savoury creamy custard.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Surprisingly, Thais don't grill much at home, although they love to buy grilled food from street vendors.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.sub-title: Thai Food Inspired by Other Cultures
programme.desc: Thai chef Pailin Chongchitnant shows us some Thai dishes that were inspired by food from other cultures.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: It’s all about street food in this Vietnamese episode.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Mary Tang cooks up Cantonese recipes perfect for days when you need comfort food.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Fun host Lisa Nguyen shows us just how versatile noodles are.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Lisa Nguyen shows us recipes from Vietnam's capital city of Hanoi.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Home cook Mary Tang shows us popular Hong Kong culinary delights.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Host Mary Tang shows us how to celebrate the Moon Festival.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Host Mary Tang cooks up traditional Cantonese dim sum recipes.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Host Mary Tang shows us four easy Cantonese seafood recipes.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.sub-title: Vietnamese Food With a French Twist
programme.desc: Vietnamese dishes with French influence.

programme.title: Up The Dish
programme.desc: Up the Dish's four chefs take a shot at dumplings, from classic Americana to upscale European!

programme.title: Up The Dish
programme.desc: Up the Dish's four chefs go raw – and raw vegan – with their variations on a classic Japanese dish.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: On today's show, baking soda, creepy dining decor, kids costumes for less, Halloween gadgets, amped sandwiches, and ask Marilyn.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: On today's show, it's an opposites attact makeover for one lucky couple with style and beauty tips, and how to wear colour.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: On today's show, etiquette FAQ, cleaning tips, reno and real estate questions, cooking solutions, and home decor questions.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: On today's show, Lynn Crawford cooks up pumpkin, DIY pet costumes, Victoria's haunted spots, and a jack-o'-lantern contest.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: On today's show, oak repairs, basic repairs, Christine Ha cooks, party throwing steps, this season buys, and retirement track.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: On today's show, it's a strong in style head-to-toe makeover for two deserving Moms, and winter boot trends for less.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: On today's show, NYC with Tommy Smythe, decor for less, DIY decor, Pottery Barn's holiday display, and quick appetizers.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: On today's show, heart attack facts, women's health risks, your HPV questions answered, and three relationship killers.

programme.desc: On today's show, foodies gift guide, front door etiquette, gift wrapping, wrap battle throwdown, and holiday brunch.

programme.desc: 7 steps for a current & stylish home; convertible furniture for small spaces; Grey’s Anatomy stars Kevin McKidd & Greg Germann.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: 7 steps for a current & stylish home; convertible furniture for small spaces; Grey’s Anatomy stars Kevin McKidd & Greg Germann.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Home cook Mary Tang shows us popular Hong Kong culinary delights.

programme.title: China's Long March Into Space
programme.desc: Three men sit atop 500 tons of liquid fuel in China's Shenzhou 7 ship, ready to blast 185 miles into space.

programme.title: Voyage of Curiosity
programme.desc: Curiosity's robot rover exploring Mars is now known as one of the greatest achievements in space exploration.

programme.title: Space Colonies
programme.desc: Space colonization may start with the Moon. Getting material there may be relatively simple. But risks exist.

programme.title: Hurricane: The Anatomy
programme.desc: This episode explores the search for hurricane scientists' holy grail.

programme.title: Hurricane: The Anatomy
programme.desc: This episode looks at how plants and animals cope with the annual onslaught in the hurricane zone.

programme.title: Hurricane: The Anatomy
programme.desc: With coastal habitats and natural defenses irretrievably damaged, the future appears bleak.

programme.title: Cosmic Journeys
programme.desc: While nature bestowed Earth with life, it enveloped deadly Venus in sulfuric acid clouds and volcanic flows.

programme.title: Atlas of a Changing Earth
programme.desc: This documentary reveals the processes melting glaciers, and explores how rising seas may threaten coastlines.

programme.title: Shockwave
programme.desc: A powerful earthquake and tsunami will some day devastate the Pacific Northwest. What can we do to prepare?

programme.title: When We Were Apollo
programme.desc: Take an intimate and personal look at Apollo through the lives and experiences of its most inspiring figures.

programme.title: Hubble's Enduring Legacy
programme.desc: The Hubble's iconic images are brought to life to answer the most important questions now facing astronomers.

programme.title: Earth in 1000 years
programme.desc: Earth's stores of ice on Greenland and Antarctica are melting, and sea levels rising. Where is Earth headed?

programme.title: Cosmic Journeys
programme.sub-title: The Largest Black Holes in the Universe
programme.desc: Black holes are mysterious. Find out what impact the massive giants have had on the Universe and its galaxies.

programme.title: Cosmic Journeys
programme.sub-title: Black Holes and the High-Energy Universe
programme.desc: Astronomers are probing the cosmic frontier to find out how the Universe works on a fundamental level.

programme.title: Cosmic Journeys
programme.desc: What will we learn about our Universe from the Voyager spacecraft's epic journey to the stars?

programme.title: Cosmic Journeys
programme.desc: Is our Earth one of the countless life-bearing worlds in our huge galaxy, or is it alone in a barren universe?

programme.title: Cosmic Journeys
programme.desc: How did matter come to exist, and what became of its birth twin, a mysterious substance called antimatter?

programme.title: Cosmic Journeys
programme.desc: Why do some hurricanes and typhoons evolve into monsters that leave in their wake trails of destruction?

programme.title: Science vs. God
programme.desc: As knowledge about the universe increases, the question becomes, is there evidence of a creator?

programme.title: Science vs. God
programme.desc: The ongoing argument is between those who believe in God and believers of evolution. Is there room for both?

programme.title: Science vs. God
programme.desc: How are we conscious beings aware of ourselves, others and the world around us? Science or God?

programme.title: Hubble: Eye in the Sky
programme.desc: In this beautiful show, viewers get an inside look at the challenges of operating the Hubble telescope.

programme.title: Space Colonies
programme.desc: Space colonization may start with the Moon. Getting material there may be relatively simple. But risks exist.

programme.title: Hurricane: The Anatomy
programme.desc: This episode explores the search for hurricane scientists' holy grail.

programme.title: Hurricane: The Anatomy
programme.desc: This episode looks at how plants and animals cope with the annual onslaught in the hurricane zone.

programme.title: Hurricane: The Anatomy
programme.desc: With coastal habitats and natural defenses irretrievably damaged, the future appears bleak.

programme.title: Cosmic Journeys
programme.desc: While nature bestowed Earth with life, it enveloped deadly Venus in sulfuric acid clouds and volcanic flows.

programme.title: Atlas of a Changing Earth
programme.desc: This documentary reveals the processes melting glaciers, and explores how rising seas may threaten coastlines.

programme.title: Living with Volcanoes
programme.desc: All around the earth, crowds pray and worship volcanoes that are sacred to them.

programme.title: Living with Volcanoes
programme.desc: Japanese fertile lands all attest of the immeasurable wealth hidden beneath the brimstone giants.

programme.title: Living with Volcanoes
programme.desc: Volcanoes have been ripping the earth apart. Are we on the verge of the next Pompeii tragedy?

programme.title: Living with Volcanoes
programme.desc: Volcanologists are ready to face any challenge to quench their thirst for knowledge

programme.title: Voyage of the Continents
programme.sub-title: Oceania: The Tectonic Ring of Fire
programme.desc: Pacific and Australian tectonic plates clash across the South Pacific causing intense volcanic activity

programme.title: Voyage of the Continents
programme.sub-title: Asia: The Tectonics of Life and Death
programme.desc: First Asia hurtled into Europe. They remain bound together by the Urals.

programme.title: Voyage of the Continents
programme.sub-title: Asia: Rising Mountains and Sinking Countries
programme.desc: As the Indo-Asian collision continued into the rest of Asia, the deforming pressure created islands.

programme.title: Voyage of the Continents
programme.desc: One billion years ago there was just one great continent on earth. Then, it began to break apart.

programme.title: Voyage of the Continents
programme.desc: Over time, the Atlantic Ocean widened. Then Africa moved north, struck Europe and birthed the Alps.

programme.title: A Wolf's Journey
programme.desc: Driven out of his pack, a young wolf has to face his fate alone and try to survive in a world ruled by man.

programme.title: Wildest Africa
programme.desc: The Okavango Delta, a huge emerald oasis is the lifeblood of this extraordinary natural Eden.

programme.title: Wildest Africa
programme.desc: Namibia is an arid, rough land. However, it shows no sign of losing popularity with animals and people.

programme.title: Wildest Africa
programme.desc: Ngorongoro is a land full of wildlife, from proud lions to vast herds of wildebeest and elephants.

programme.title: Wildest Africa
programme.desc: Zambezi’s long and winding journey destroys, as well as creates.

programme.title: Wildest Africa
programme.desc: Ancient Egypt, one of the history’s most powerful civilizations, depended on the Nile.

programme.title: Carousel of Life
programme.desc: The orchard is a unique ecosystem. As seasons change, we experience the interplay of nature, man, and animal.

programme.title: Strange Creatures of the Arctic
programme.desc: Explore adaptations that help five species thrive, and the challenges they face to survive in extreme cold.

programme.title: A Wolf's Journey
programme.desc: Driven out of his pack, a young wolf has to face his fate alone and try to survive in a world ruled by man.

programme.title: Planet E: Fixing Earth from your Backyard
programme.sub-title: On Silent Paws: The Return of the Lynx
programme.desc: The lynx was tragically hunted to the brink of extinction.

programme.title: Planet E: Fixing Earth from your Backyard
programme.sub-title: The Nuclear Threat: Europe's Accident-prone Reactors
programme.desc: Older nuclear power stations are still allowed to continue running. Are we sitting on a nuclear powder keg?

programme.title: Planet E: Fixing Earth from your Backyard
programme.desc: More than 2 million animals are used for experiments in Germany annually. Is there no alternative to this?

programme.title: The Nature Effect
programme.desc: Experiencing nature stimulates unexpected biological and psychological resources, heightening your well-being.

programme.title: Our Roots, Our Future
programme.desc: Scientists are studying root growth. Roots may help solve climate change and protect the environment.

programme.title: In the Mind of Plants
programme.desc: Can plants sense pain? Some scientists are convinced plants have surpassed animals on their evolutionary path.

programme.title: Evolution Earth
programme.desc: From the earth to the stars to the edge of the unknown, explore the mystery, majesty and power of the world we live in and beyond.

programme.title: Evolution Earth
programme.desc: From the earth to the stars to the edge of the unknown, explore the mystery, majesty and power of the world we live in and beyond.

programme.title: Evolution Earth
programme.desc: From the earth to the stars to the edge of the unknown, explore the mystery, majesty and power of the world we live in and beyond.

programme.title: Wild & Wide Awake - From the Mediterranean to the Alps
programme.desc: The first part of the spring journey takes us from the Spanish inland to the rugged Atlantic and to the Mediterranean, through the French Provence, and finally into the Alps. Along the way, flamingos perform their bizarre dancing rituals, wild horses storm through the wetland… While the North is still covered by snow and ice, the South will soon turn into a blooming garden and every creature is enjoying the first signs of the awakening.

programme.title: Asia - Secret Lives Hidden Places II
programme.sub-title: Asia - Secret Lives Hidden Places II: Gir National Park: India – Asia’s Last Lion
programme.desc: In the far west of India, unexpected predators roam: lions. Living freely in a human-inhabited area without fences, they occasionally prey on the livestock of the indigenous Maldhari people, who nevertheless take pride in sharing their region with these lions—a unique coexistence of humans and predators. In northern Gujarat, desert landscapes dominate, where semi-nomadic communities breed a unique camel breed capable of swimming: Karai camels swim up to 3km daily to mangrove islands for grazing.

programme.title: Asia - Secret Lives Hidden Places II
programme.sub-title: Asia - Secret Lives Hidden Places II: Kinabatangan River: Borneo – Forgotten Paradise
programme.desc: In Sabah, northern Borneo, flows the 560km-long Kinabatangan River. Nowhere else in Sabah are wild animals as easily spotted as along this river: orangutans, elephants, crocodiles, and proboscis monkeys. However, biodiversity is threatened as more rainforest gives way to palm oil plantations. Wildlife ventures into inhabited areas in search of food, leading to conflicts. Locals are fighting to preserve the rainforest and its unique wildlife, demonstrating that conservation and prosperity can coexist even in a modern society.

programme.title: Asia - Secret Lives Hidden Places II
programme.sub-title: Asia - Secret Lives Hidden Places II: Pu Mat National Park: Vietnam – Of Bears and Men
programme.desc: The jungle in northern Vietnam is nearly impenetrable, with some areas never touched by humans. The remote rainforests of Pu Mat National Park serve as a refuge for endangered wildlife such as black bears, monkeys, and pangolins. Tuan, a biologist and bear specialist, embarks on an expedition to these untouched regions of the park in search of bears. The most hunted animal here is the pangolin, sought after for its scales believed to have medicinal properties and its meat considered a delicacy. A small Vietnamese conservation organization fights for the survival of this species.

programme.title: Asia - Secret Lives Hidden Places II
programme.sub-title: Asia - Secret Lives Hidden Places II: Gaoligongshan Park: China – Himalaya’s Gold
programme.desc: The Great Gobi National Park in southwestern Mongolia shelters one of the last populations of wild Bactrian camels, amid its ancient and mysterious desert landscape. With around 800 critically endangered animals, the Gobi remains one of the world's most sparsely inhabited and inhospitable regions, yet its allure is undeniable, offering a captivating backdrop for encounters with wild camels and stories of people deeply connected to nature.

programme.title: Asia - Secret Lives Hidden Places II
programme.sub-title: Asia - Secret Lives Hidden Places II: Great Gobi National Park: Mongolia – Following the Wild Camels
programme.desc: The jungle in northern Vietnam is nearly impenetrable, with some areas never touched by humans. The remote rainforests of Pu Mat National Park serve as a refuge for endangered wildlife such as black bears, monkeys, and pangolins. Tuan, a biologist and bear specialist, embarks on an expedition to these untouched regions of the park in search of bears. The most hunted animal here is the pangolin, sought after for its scales believed to have medicinal properties and its meat considered a delicacy. A small Vietnamese conservation organization fights for the survival of this species.

programme.title: Dreams of the Settlers - An American Journey
programme.sub-title: Through Utah's Canyons to Salt Lake City
programme.desc: It’s a dream route leading from the casino metropolis of Las Vegas, to Salt Lake City, the Mormon capital. It is a journey that follows the footsteps of the first settlers: the Mormons. They came to this desert belt of the United States during the second half of the 19th century to escape persecution and in search of the promised land. Some of them founded Las Vegas. Others moved through the canyons and over the mountains of the colossal Colorado Plateau, where they found water and pasture, to establish many small settlements. The landscapes through which they trekked, had and still have, a breathtaking and bizarre majestic beauty. Bryce Canyon, the hogbacks and the Escalante Staircase plateau are dramatic scenes of wonder formed from rock. The Pajute Indians once described Bryce Canyon as: “red rocks standing like men in a basin”.

programme.title: Dreams of the Settlers - An American Journey
programme.desc: The vast Delta of the Mississippi River is the gateway to the South of the United States. It always has been. This is where our journey begins: through Louisiana along the banks of the mighty river, to the big inland city of New Orleans, founded long ago by the French. Further inland, sugarcane plantations and the villas of their former owners stretch as far as the eye can see. To the north, we leave the river and, taking spectacular roads, we pass through the swamplands of the Mississippi, which were once impenetrable, serving as the ideal hideout for many a runaway. Bordering Texas, where the swamplands end, is the beginning of the vast cattle country. This is home to the last remaining cowboys in the United States who, still today, maintain their French cultural heritage.

programme.title: Dreams of the Settlers - An American Journey
programme.sub-title: Along the Shores of the Great Lakes
programme.desc: The Great Lakes are among the most mythical landscapes of North America: endless water, endless forest. Many Native American peoples lived there; later European settlers followed on the heels of the white hunters and trappers. They hunted in the forests and transported their furs and other goods across the water to the south where a small river situated at the southern tip of Lake Michigan flows onward towards the southern states. The settlement that sprung up there was called Checagou by the Indians. Much later, the settlement was to be the first skyscraper city in the world: Chicago, the megacity on the Great Lake.

programme.title: The marvellous World of French Rivers
programme.desc: The Gironde is the largest and most powerful tidal current in Europe. When the Gironde flows into the Atlantic, it is 15 kilometers wide and in the rhythm of the tides, it brings the waters from the Pyrenees and the Massif Central into the ocean. This often pushes its waters far out into the Gironde at high tide.This creates a world of its own that has shaped everything uniquely and intensely for tens of thousands of years. The water surface of the river is so large that the evaporation has created its own microclimate, which is stable and, together with the brackish waters, has influenced flora and fauna on the right and left of the bank for a long time.

programme.title: The marvellous World of French Rivers
programme.desc: The Dordogne consists of a visible and an invisible system of flowing water. Caves and basins under the earth's surface store the excess water and return it to the river when it is dry. A perfect management of a precious resource, not invented by humans, but by the complex nature itself. The result is an ever life-giving river.

programme.title: Chiemgau, A Changing Landscape
programme.desc: A few thousand years ago, there were bare moraine ridges and barren meltwater valleys here. A landscape born in the last ice age, characterized by the edge of the Alps, gentle hills, moors, and lakes. And by cultural richness, not only on the islands in Lake Chiemsee, with their famous monasteries and castles. Long before King Ludwig II, the first people settled in the Chiemgau region. Finds of hunting weapons from the Neanderthal era prove that the land was inhabited even during the ice age, probably because there was plenty of game to hunt. During the Bronze Age, one of the most important trade routes from the mountains to the Alpine foothills passed through here. Then came the Celts and the Romans.

programme.title: Chiemgau, A Changing Landscape
programme.desc: Three times in the past two decades, the Chiemsee area has experienced "once-in-a-century" floods. Climate, wildlife, and landscape have changed, and it seems this transformation is accelerating. Species living in the flood zones are developing sophisticated survival strategies. Knot ants, for instance, place their queen in the middle and form a raft with their bodies, even conquering new habitats. Flooding is less of a disaster for nature and impacts humans more significantly.

programme.title: Wild Faces of Switzerland - Winter in the Swiss Alps
programme.desc: In the heart of Europe lies Switzerland. It’s a surprisingly big little country: small in area, but big on natural beauty. And, like its many languages and cultures, its scenic diversity is something really special, too: from mighty Alps, dense forests and lush green valleys, to mysterious moors and roaring waterfalls. Mediterranean lakes and craggy rock landscapes are all close together in one small geographical location. Switzerland has many wild faces – some familiar and some hidden. It’s a country that’s astonishingly rich in variety, wilderness and scenic wonder. Each episode of the series seeks to do justice to this diversity.

programme.title: Wild & Wide Awake - From the Mediterranean to the Alps
programme.desc: The first part of the spring journey takes us from the Spanish inland to the rugged Atlantic and to the Mediterranean, through the French Provence, and finally into the Alps. Along the way, flamingos perform their bizarre dancing rituals, wild horses storm through the wetland… While the North is still covered by snow and ice, the South will soon turn into a blooming garden and every creature is enjoying the first signs of the awakening.

programme.title: Asia - Secret Lives Hidden Places II
programme.sub-title: Asia - Secret Lives Hidden Places II: Gir National Park: India – Asia’s Last Lion
programme.desc: In the far west of India, unexpected predators roam: lions. Living freely in a human-inhabited area without fences, they occasionally prey on the livestock of the indigenous Maldhari people, who nevertheless take pride in sharing their region with these lions—a unique coexistence of humans and predators. In northern Gujarat, desert landscapes dominate, where semi-nomadic communities breed a unique camel breed capable of swimming: Karai camels swim up to 3km daily to mangrove islands for grazing.

programme.title: Asia - Secret Lives Hidden Places II
programme.sub-title: Asia - Secret Lives Hidden Places II: Kinabatangan River: Borneo – Forgotten Paradise
programme.desc: In Sabah, northern Borneo, flows the 560km-long Kinabatangan River. Nowhere else in Sabah are wild animals as easily spotted as along this river: orangutans, elephants, crocodiles, and proboscis monkeys. However, biodiversity is threatened as more rainforest gives way to palm oil plantations. Wildlife ventures into inhabited areas in search of food, leading to conflicts. Locals are fighting to preserve the rainforest and its unique wildlife, demonstrating that conservation and prosperity can coexist even in a modern society.

programme.title: Asia - Secret Lives Hidden Places II
programme.sub-title: Asia - Secret Lives Hidden Places II: Pu Mat National Park: Vietnam – Of Bears and Men
programme.desc: The jungle in northern Vietnam is nearly impenetrable, with some areas never touched by humans. The remote rainforests of Pu Mat National Park serve as a refuge for endangered wildlife such as black bears, monkeys, and pangolins. Tuan, a biologist and bear specialist, embarks on an expedition to these untouched regions of the park in search of bears. The most hunted animal here is the pangolin, sought after for its scales believed to have medicinal properties and its meat considered a delicacy. A small Vietnamese conservation organization fights for the survival of this species.

programme.title: Asia - Secret Lives Hidden Places II
programme.sub-title: Asia - Secret Lives Hidden Places II: Gaoligongshan Park: China – Himalaya’s Gold
programme.desc: The Great Gobi National Park in southwestern Mongolia shelters one of the last populations of wild Bactrian camels, amid its ancient and mysterious desert landscape. With around 800 critically endangered animals, the Gobi remains one of the world's most sparsely inhabited and inhospitable regions, yet its allure is undeniable, offering a captivating backdrop for encounters with wild camels and stories of people deeply connected to nature.

programme.title: Asia - Secret Lives Hidden Places II
programme.sub-title: Asia - Secret Lives Hidden Places II: Great Gobi National Park: Mongolia – Following the Wild Camels
programme.desc: The jungle in northern Vietnam is nearly impenetrable, with some areas never touched by humans. The remote rainforests of Pu Mat National Park serve as a refuge for endangered wildlife such as black bears, monkeys, and pangolins. Tuan, a biologist and bear specialist, embarks on an expedition to these untouched regions of the park in search of bears. The most hunted animal here is the pangolin, sought after for its scales believed to have medicinal properties and its meat considered a delicacy. A small Vietnamese conservation organization fights for the survival of this species.

programme.title: Cuba - Brilliantly Caribbean
programme.desc: Even anyone who has not yet been to Cuba can conjure up certain images of American vintage street cruisers, imposing buildings that have seen better days, white sandy beaches full of palms and loving couples on the Malecón against a backdrop of crashing waves. This island is somewhat of a myth and its people generate a special joie de vivre. The legendary Hershey Train departs for Matanzas from the capital, Havana. On the oldest electrified railway line in the Caribbean, 60-year-old Spanish carriages make their way along al-most 100 kilometres of coastline in the most relaxed way possible. The Salvavidas on Varadero, probably the most famous beach on the island, have a particular-ly beautiful workplace. The lifeguards' territory ex-tends for more than 20 kilometres. In order to protect the coasts of Cuba, mangroves have been replanted here for many years. The project is supported by the UN and planting is often carried out by schoolchildren. In doing so, Cuban children learn how to treat their maritime habitat in a sustainable fashion. The former colonial city of Trinidad on Cuba's south coast is gen-erally considered to be one of the most beautiful places in the entire Caribbean. The houses here are particular-ly colourful. A plethora of magnificent buildings can be seen at the Plaza Mayor, the neo-baroque main square.

programme.title: mareTV: The Caribbean for explorers: St. Vincent and the Grenadines
programme.desc: Much more Caribbean than on the islands of St. Vincent and the Grenadines is hardly possible. Deserted beaches, uninhabited islands, lush vegetation, the 33 islands in total are dreamlike with extremely hospitable inhabitants. Miranda Phillips runs a laundry and water boat on Bequia. Every morning, the 32-year-old navigates from yacht to yacht with her small bright green service vehicle and is already expected by most skippers. Miranda promises radiantly fresh laundry, which she delivers again the same day. And so there is always something going on in her tiny laundrette in the harbour. It turns into a karaoke bar in the evening, because that is Miranda's great passion. Eban Olliver has many jobs at once. He is the lighthouse keeper of Kingstown. And that means a lot of responsibility in St. Vincent's biggest port city. Because Eban doesn't just change the light bulbs, he's a pilot dispatcher, harbour master and guard all in one person. First contact with ships is particularly important. Once he even saved the entire nation from an Ebola virus.

programme.title: mareTV: Peru - Coast of the Incas
programme.desc: Dramatically, the coast plunges into the sea, the so-called Costa of Peru is a gigantic desert on the Pacific Ocean, 2,400 kilometers long. Rugged, bizarre, like from another star. Ancient cultures settled here, the Moche and the Chimú. Later the proud Inca, the self-proclaimed royal people of the Andes, ruled. In the small town of Huanchaco in northern Peru, the fishermen build a new boat every month: from reed. The small ships are called Caballito de Totora (reed horses), because the shape is reminiscent of a horse and the fishermen ride in it kneeling in the waves. About 2,000 years ago, the moche used it to go out to sea. Carlos Segura Azola is a descendant of the moche. He is currently building a "reed horse" again. But actually something completely different occupies him. Carlos has been afraid of the sea ever since his father drowned at work. The fisherman consults a Brucha, a witch. She tries to drive him out of panic at the Pacific with mysterious ceremonies. At some point Carlos' reed boat is finished and he has to get out to his nets again. In the south, in the port city of Pisco, there are hardly any private cars. Mototaxis on three wheels are the means of transport here. Every moto looks different, because the young drivers compete with each other. They constantly pimp out their tricycles with spoilers, mirrors and all kinds of decorations. Because if you stand out in the traffic jam, you get the most passengers. But nowhere else in Peru is road traffic as dense and chaotic as in the capital Lima. That's why Juan Ortiz goes daily with his Delta glider over the coastal road into the air. He is the flying reporter and reports live for the traffic news. And he is not alone with his aircraft. Dozens of paragliders float along the cliffs of Lima at any time of the day. The Pacific provides a constant upwind on the up to 160 meter high cliffs. Somewhere in the labyrinth of the metropolis of millions hundreds of believers trot to the beat of the music of a Jesus statue on the cross. A procession for the "Lord of Miracles", the brass band intones church songs. The music student Bruno Alvarado drags and plays the largest instrument, a sousaphone. Every day there are dozens of processions in Lima. Practical for Bruno, he finances his studies with the musical accompaniment. Away from the hustle and bustle of the big city, in the Paracas National Park, it is deserted. Luis Zapato runs a kiosk on a lonely beach amidst huge sand dunes. The kiosk has meanwhile developed into a gourmet temple, even the capital city dwellers flock here. Especially because of the scallops that Luis is the only licence holder to collect far and wide from the seabed. Inti Mar he called his beach snack, Inti is the sun god, Mar the sea. At Luis you literally eat like a sun god at the sea. This is only possible here, on the Inca coast.

programme.title: The U.S. East Coast
programme.sub-title: The U.S. East Coast, The City and the Sea
programme.desc: The journey up the coast continues through Delaware to suburban New Jersey, and onwards into the heart of Manhattan. In Delaware, famous as a corporate tax haven, we visit a winery. In New York City we zip around with a bicycle courier and then head out to Long Island for oysters.

programme.title: The U.S. East Coast
programme.sub-title: The U.S. East Coast, Florida - The Sunshine State
programme.desc: Nowhere is the American way of life as relaxed as on Florida's beaches. The Keys, a chain of islands at the far southern end of the state, reveal a more laid-back side of the USA. But all of Florida amazes -- with secluded beaches, unspoiled nature, and the legacy of the early European settlers.

programme.title: The U.S. East Coast
programme.sub-title: The U.S. East Coast, The Heart of New England
programme.desc: From New York, the journey up the coast leads into the heart of New England. We visit Yale; spend some time in the 19th century mansions at Newport, RI; tour the coastal fishing and vacation towns of Massachusetts, and then meet a modern entrepreneur in Boston and see her rooftop gardens.

programme.title: The U.S. East Coast
programme.sub-title: The U.S. East Coast, Surf And Turf
programme.desc: New Hampshire has only 19 kilometers of coastline and short summers, all the more reason Shauna Fraser takes every opportunity to be by the sea. In the sheltered harbor of Rye, Shauna also targets inner attitude with yoga on a paddleboard.

programme.title: Ibex: Acrobats On The Rocks
programme.desc: The film follows Buck, an Alpine Ibex from Italy's Gran Paradiso, highlighting his life and majestic beauty.

programme.title: Trees Of Life
programme.desc: See how humble plants triggered Earth’s greatest transformation

programme.title: Giant Grizzlies
programme.desc: Join Andreas Kieling and a native Alaskan on a dangerous expedition through Alaska’s grizzly territories.

programme.title: Unbelievable Beasts
programme.desc: Discover the incredible world of extraordinary animals like a half-tonne tiger and lion hybrid

programme.title: Unbelievable Beasts
programme.desc: Explore the survival strategies of animals like cane toads, frogs, and chameleons through expert insights.

programme.title: Conflicts of Nature
programme.desc: The water beetle emerges in spring, battling for survival in a pond with various creatures.

programme.title: Conflicts of Nature
programme.desc: In French Guyana's Amazon, a young jaguar faces fierce warrior ants, revealing jungle drama.

programme.title: Conflicts of Nature
programme.desc: In Kenya's Masai Mara, olive baboons endure floods, rivalries, and attacks, showcasing survival.

programme.title: Conflicts of Nature
programme.desc: At Murchison Falls, monitor lizards raid Nile crocodile nests, while crocs seek revenge.

programme.title: Magical Reef: The Islands of the Four Kings
programme.desc: Explore Indonesia's underwater paradise, home to vibrant coral reefs and unique sea life species.

programme.title: Evolution
programme.desc: This documentary explores life's emergence, wildlife, habitats, and adaptation across continents.

programme.title: Wild Italy: Top Predators
programme.desc: The wolf is performing a remarkable comeback in the mountains of Italy.

programme.title: Wild Italy: Top Predators
programme.desc: No one expected the outcome of introducing seven Slovenian bears into Trentino's Adamello woods.

programme.title: Wild Italy: Top Predators
programme.desc: Two eagles struggle to survive and produce offspring to maintain their species.

programme.title: Mystery Monkeys Of Shangri-La
programme.desc: A two-year film team documents orphaned snub-nosed monkeys in Yunnan, showing love and loss.

programme.title: Life On Fire
programme.desc: Animals in New Britain, Papua New Guinea, adapt to Earth's changing moods and conditions.

programme.title: Life On Fire
programme.desc: The study explores how sooty terns and Alvin shrimp adapt to the harsh Tongan archipelago.

programme.title: Conflicts of Nature
programme.desc: The water beetle emerges in spring, battling for survival in a pond with various creatures.

programme.title: Conflicts of Nature
programme.desc: In French Guyana's Amazon, a young jaguar faces fierce warrior ants, revealing jungle drama.

programme.title: Conflicts of Nature
programme.desc: In Kenya's Masai Mara, olive baboons endure floods, rivalries, and attacks, showcasing survival.

programme.title: Conflicts of Nature
programme.desc: At Murchison Falls, monitor lizards raid Nile crocodile nests, while crocs seek revenge.

programme.desc: Explore Indonesia's underwater paradise, home to vibrant coral reefs and unique sea life species.

programme.title: Evolution
programme.desc: This documentary explores life's emergence, wildlife, habitats, and adaptation across continents.

programme.title: Wild Italy: Top Predators
programme.desc: The wolf is performing a remarkable comeback in the mountains of Italy.

programme.title: Wild Italy: Top Predators
